subject: [PATCH v2 08/14] userfaultfd: add UFFDIO_REGISTER_MODE_RWP and UFFDIO_RWPROTECT plumbing

reviews.author: Mike Rapoport
reviews.summary:
Gave Reviewed-by

Reviewer suggested a minor improvement to the error handling code, recommending a boolean variable 'found' and a simple if-statement to handle the case where no VMA is found in the range, making the code more readable.
reviews.raw_body:
It's an interesting way to say "no VMA found in range" :)
I think bool found and
	
	if (!found)
		return -ENOENT;

looks more readable.

-- 
Sincerely yours,
Mike.

2026-05-12.patch_summary: This patch adds a new feature to the userfaultfd interface, allowing userspace to track read-write protection (RWP) on registered memory ranges. It introduces two new ioctls: UFFDIO_REGISTER_MODE_RWP and UFFDIO_RWPROTECT, which enable RWP tracking and installation/removal of RWP on a range. The patch also includes plumbing for the feature, including capability bits and VMA flags. The goal is to prevent RWP state from outliving the uffd and ensure that re-registering a range does not drop modes that install per-PTE markers.
